issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (line 651) and applies a translateY(1px) transform on :active (line 663). issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on forced-colors :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query removes the outline for :focus-visible without providing a visible alternative. In high-contrast mode, this leaves users with no focus indicator at all.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users on all interactive elements, making it impossible to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px (24–43px range) with no padding compensation, potentially providing insufficient touch target area for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule and only changes the outline-offset. Without setting a visible outline style, no focus ring appears even when the class is applied.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto instead of position: absolute and 1px dimensions, failing to visually hide content while keeping it accessible. This makes the content visible to all users and potentially breaks layout.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has an explicit height of 28px (24–43px range) with no compensating padding to ensure a 44px touch target area, potentially causing activation issues for some users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon-only button has min-width and min-height of 20px, below the 24px minimum requirement, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links remove text-decoration without providing an alternative non-colour visual differentiator (such as underline on hover, border-bottom, or background highlight), relying solely on colour change to distinguish links from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the default focus outline without providing a visible alternative, making it impossible for keyboard users to see when the field is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, preventing keyboard users from identifying the focused link.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the focus outline without replacement, leaving no visible indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus state sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring for keyboard users to identify the focused button.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has width and height of 20px, below the 24px minimum requirement, making it difficult for users with motor impairments or tremors to select accurately.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule. Setting only outline-color does not restore visibility when outline width is zero, leaving no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum requirement of 24px for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px (24–43px range) with padding set to 0, resulting in a touch target smaller than the recommended 44px without adequate compensation.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden-focusable utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only-focusable class uses incorrect positioning properties (static/auto) that fail to hide content visually, and the focus-within state may not properly restore visibility for skip links.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button component includes transform in its transition property (used for the :active translateY effect) but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override, potentially causing issues for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transform transition for hover effects, and the existing prefers-reduced-motion media query at lines 522-525 is empty, failing to disable the motion animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link uses a transform transition for the slide-in animation but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override, which may cause discomfort for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA primary button
issuesFound.explanation: The hero primary button includes transform in its transition (used for hover translateY effect) without a reduced-motion override, potentially triggering vestibular symptoms.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 190: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Button active state transform lacks reduced-motion support
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition and applies a translateY transform on :active, but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this motion for users sensitive to animation.

issuesFound.title: Button touch target below recommended size

issuesFound.explanation: The primary hero button translates vertically on hover with a transition, but no reduced-motion media query disables this spatial movement.

issuesFound.title: Skip link transition lacks reduced-motion support
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link uses a transform transition to animate into view, which can cause vestibular issues for users with motion sensitivity and lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override.

issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which creates negative word spacing. This reduces the space between words and can significantly impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, as well as those using magnification.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout and fails to hide it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .stat-label foreground rgba(255,255,255,0.3) on background #5588bb (ratio below 3:1)
issuesFound.explanation: The stat label uses semi-transparent white text (30% opacity) on a gradient background. Against the darker brand primary color (#5588bb), the contrast is approximately 2.1:1, and against the lighter secondary color it is even worse. This fails WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum).
issuesFound.fix: Line 891: .stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .social-links a foreground #94a3b8 on background #555555 (ratio 2.4:1)
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links in the footer use the same low-contrast colours (#94a3b8 on #555555), resulting in a 2.4:1 contrast ratio that fails WCAG 1.4.3 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1182: .social-links a { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-200); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); min-height: 28px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .footer-brand address a foreground #94a3b8 on background #555555 (ratio 2.4:1)
issuesFound.explanation: Address links in the footer brand section use colour-neutral-400 (#94a3b8) on the dark footer background (#555555), resulting in insufficient contrast for readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 977: .footer-brand address a { color: var(--colour-neutral-200); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); display: inline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation menu links have text-decoration: none (inherited from line 319) and rely solely on colour change on hover (to colour-brand-primary) without an underline or other non-colour visual differentiator. Users with colour vision deficiencies may not distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 535: #nav-menu > li > a:hover, #nav-menu > li > button:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-info-light); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links rely solely on colour change on hover without text decoration or other non-colour indicators, making them indistinguishable from text for users with certain colour vision deficiencies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 600: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use text-decoration: none and only change colour on hover, failing to provide a non-colour visual indicator required by WCAG 1.4.1 for links within body text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 989: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus indicator, making it impossible for keyboard users to identify which element is focused. outline-offset alone does not create a visible ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible (forced-colors)
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, :focus-visible sets outline: none without a visible alternative. This breaks focus visibility specifically in high-contrast modes where users rely on system colors.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline without replacing it with a box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating through buttons will lose visual focus indication.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link's focus state removes the outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when the logo link is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the outline on focus without a visible alternative. This hides focus state from keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot track focus within dropdown menus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the outline on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on this interactive element.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users lose focus visibility in this region.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the outline on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus on these links.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the active filter control.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove the outline on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see which billing period is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the scrollable region.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove the outline on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see which question is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on form inputs.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove the outline on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users lose focus visibility in the footer.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot track focus in footer nav.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove the outline on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on these links.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px. With padding (6px), the total height is 32px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px. With padding (10px), the total size is 40px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to 28px × 28px with no padding. This is below the 24px minimum and 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is explicitly sized to 20px × 20px. This is below the 24px minimum touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer nav links have min-height: 24px with no compensating padding defined in this rule. This falls in the 24–43px range without spacing to reach 44px.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility: .sr-only
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually while keeping it for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 523 is empty and does not disable this motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can significantly impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p text uses #aaaaaa on a #ffffff background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.3:1, which fails the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer nav links have text-decoration: none and only change color on hover. Users who cannot perceive color differences may not identify these as links.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social links have text-decoration: none and only change color on hover. Non-colour visual differentiators are required to identify them as links.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the field is linked to an error message via `aria-describedby` but `aria-invalid="true"` is never set, so screen readers do not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1236 (after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`): Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the trigger button's `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to "true". Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1770 (after `_open = true;`): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown panel updates CSS classes but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button, preventing screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1930 (after `inst.open = true;`): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding an FAQ item does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button, so screen readers do not convey the expanded state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3430 (after `item.open = true;`): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The focus trap is instantiated during initialization but `activate()` is never called when the menu opens. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer and lose focus context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1782 (after `_lockScroll();`): Add `_trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger button accessible name not ensured
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle is enhanced with ARIA states but the code does not verify or set an accessible name, which is required for screen readers to identify the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1722 (after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`): Add `if (_trigger && !_trigger.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_trigger.textContent.trim()) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.labels.openMenu || 'Open navigation menu');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in the `mobileNav` module toggles the navigation drawer visibility but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1273: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function in the `mobileNav` module hides the navigation drawer but does not announce the change to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technology are not notified when the menu closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1299: if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function in the `dropdownMenu` module reveals the dropdown panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. This leaves the ARIA state out of sync with the visual state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1437: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function changes visible content (shows the panel) but does not write to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not notified when a dropdown opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1440: liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu opened');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function hides the dropdown panel but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users are not informed when the dropdown closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1456: liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in the `faqAccordion` module expands the answer panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the question button. The ARIA state remains incorrect.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2802: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function collapses the FAQ answer but does not announce this change via a live region. Screen reader users are not notified when an answer collapses.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2848: if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question)); }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` utility function adds an error description but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field. Assistive technologies rely on this attribute to identify invalid inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 937: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the navigation drawer by adding CSS classes and locking scroll, but it never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce that the menu is expanded, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu click opening lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened via mouse click, focus remains on the trigger button and no live region announcement is made. Unlike keyboard navigation (which moves focus into the panel), mouse users relying on screen readers receive no confirmation that the panel opened.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened, the trigger button's `aria-expanded` attribute is not updated to "true". The `_closeInstance` function correctly sets it to "false", but the open path is missing the corresponding true state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding an FAQ answer panel does not update the trigger button's `aria-expanded` attribute to "true". The `close` function sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry where screen readers cannot detect when an item is expanded.

issuesFound.title: productFilter view mode buttons missing initial aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The grid/list view toggle buttons rely on `aria-pressed` to indicate state, but this attribute is only set on click in `_onViewToggle`. On initial load, the buttons lack `aria-pressed`, so screen readers do not know which view is active.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer does not trap focus
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer acts as a modal but never activates a focus trap. The `_trap` variable is declared but never assigned. Keyboard users can Tab out of the drawer into the background content, violating WCAG 2.4.3 and 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid` function marks a field as invalid visually and updates `aria-describedby`, but it fails to set `aria-invalid="true"`. Screen readers will not announce that the field is in an error state, preventing users from understanding why validation failed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 937: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function sets the drawer to visible but does not update the trigger button's `aria-expanded` attribute to "true". The `close` function sets it to "false", creating a state mismatch where the button always reports "collapsed" even when the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1318: _open = true; setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav close function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close` function hides the navigation drawer but does not announce this state change to screen readers via a live region. The `open` function announces "Navigation menu opened", but closing is silent, leaving users unsure if the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1354: emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer }); liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance` function reveals the dropdown panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. This prevents screen reader users from knowing the menu is currently expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1465: inst.open = true; setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown close function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._closeInstance` function hides the dropdown panel but does not announce the state change to screen readers. Users relying on audio feedback are not informed that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1490: emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel }); liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` function expands the answer panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the question trigger button. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the button's state does not match its visual presentation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2659: item.open = true; setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.close` function collapses the answer panel but does not announce this action to screen readers. While opening announces "expanded", closing is silent, degrading the experience for non-visual users.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent accept action does not announce confirmation
issuesFound.explanation: The `cookieConsent._onAccept` function saves preferences and hides the banner but does not announce that preferences were saved. Users are left without confirmation that their choice was registered.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent decline action does not announce confirmation
issuesFound.explanation: The `cookieConsent._onDecline` function saves preferences and hides the banner but does not announce that preferences were saved. Users are left without confirmation that their choice was registered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3650: analytics.revokeConsent(); liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookies.saved || 'Cookie preferences saved.'); _hideBanner();

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not set aria-expanded="true"

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not set aria-expanded="true"

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing Enter/Space keyboard handler for non-button triggers
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ triggers are not native <button> or <summary> elements, the script assigns them role="button" and tabindex="0", but the keyboard handler only manages Arrow, Home, and End keys. It omits Enter and Space, meaning keyboard users cannot activate the accordion toggle, violating WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"

issuesFound.fix: Line 1543 (after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl)): field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Collapsing an FAQ item updates the DOM but fails to announce the collapsed state via the live region, unlike the open function which does announce. This creates an inconsistent experience.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation

Keyboard focus can escape the drawer and move to background elements, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order and 2.4.7 Focus Visible.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function within the `mobileNav` module adds the open class and removes the hidden attribute from the drawer, but it fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the menu is now expanded, as the state is only updated on close.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not announce menu closure via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function within the `mobileNav` module sets `aria-expanded="false"` but does not announce the state change to screen readers via the `liveRegion`. Users are informed when the menu opens, but not when it closes, creating an inconsistent experience.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not announce menu open via live region

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance function does not announce menu close via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function hides the dropdown panel but does not announce this change to screen readers. While it updates `aria-expanded`, the lack of a live region announcement means users are not explicitly told the menu has closed.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function within the `faqAccordion` module expands the answer panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close` function correctly sets it to "false", resulting in an incorrect state for assistive technology when the item is open.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close function does not announce answer collapse via live region

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The close function in the mobileNav module removes the open class from the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu is now collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1666: function close(announceToSR) { ... setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init function identifies the trigger and drawer elements but does not set the aria-controls attribute on the trigger to reference the drawer's ID. This breaks the programmatic association between the button and the menu it controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1716: function init() { ... const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'main-nav'); setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', drawerId); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function in the dropdownMenu module removes the hidden attribute from the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1823: function _openInstance(inst) { ... setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function in the dropdownMenu module adds the hidden attribute to the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the dropdown is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1843: function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { ... setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open function in the faqAccordion module expands the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2646: function open(item, announceToSR) { ... setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function adds the error description via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Assistive technologies rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field has an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1376: function markInvalid(field, errorEl) { ... field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes the error description but fails to remove aria-invalid or set it to "false". The field may remain announced as invalid even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1391: function markValid(field, errorEl) { ... field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); ... }

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce count change
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function updates the product count badge text dynamically but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the number of visible products changes.

issuesFound.explanation: The _updateSavingsMessage function updates the savings message text but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the savings message appears or changes.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The play function updates the aria-pressed state of the button but does not announce the state change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that auto-play has started.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel pause does not announce state change

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); immediately before or after the addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); line.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is opened, the function adds CSS classes to display the drawer but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen readers will not announce the expanded state change to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); immediately after the line _open = true;

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering products and updating the visible grid, the function updates the count badge and emits a custom event but does not announce the filter results (e.g., "5 products found") to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside the filterAnimation.run callback, after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches);, add: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the function updates the internal state and CSS classes but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving assistive technologies with stale state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); immediately after the line _open = false;

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods (monthly/yearly), the price values update dynamically across plan cards, but the change is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that prices have changed unless they manually check each card.
issuesFound.fix: Line: After the forEach loop that updates plan cards, add: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: productFilter.init missing initial aria-pressed on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The initialization calls _syncTabAriaStates to set initial visual states, but that function does not set aria-pressed (see Issue 12), leaving the initial pressed state of the filter tabs uncommunicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Ensure _syncTabAriaStates sets aria-pressed as described in Issue 12, or explicitly initialize here: if (_tabsEl) qsa('[data-filter-value]', _tabsEl).forEach(btn => setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(btn.getAttribute('data-filter-value') === _productFilterState.activeCategory)));

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing initial aria-expanded and aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The initialization obtains references to the trigger and drawer elements but does not establish the required ARIA relationship. It fails to set the initial aria-expanded="false" on the trigger and does not set aria-controls to reference the drawer element's ID.
issuesFound.fix: Line: After _drawer = qs(...);, add: if (_trigger && _drawer) { setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav')); }

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding a FAQ item reveals the answer content but does not announce the expansion or the content to screen readers via a live region, assuming the user will notice the change. While focus management helps, the state change should be announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line: After item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');, add: if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} answer expanded`);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu instance sets the open state flag and displays the panel, but does not update aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button to communicate the expanded state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); immediately after inst.open = true;

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu updates the internal state and hides the panel after a timeout, but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, causing assistive technologies to report the menu as still expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); immediately after inst.open = false;

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding a FAQ item sets the internal state and animates the panel open, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This is inconsistent with the close() function which correctly sets it to "false", leaving screen readers unaware when content is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); immediately after item.open = true;

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearance
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing the invalid state from a field, the function removes the error message reference from aria-describedby but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute (either by setting it to "false" or removing it), causing screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Insert removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID); at the end of the function (or setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');).

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons indicate the active category via CSS class but do not set aria-pressed="true" on the active button (or "false" on inactive buttons). Since these buttons act as toggle buttons for the filter categories, they must communicate the pressed/selected state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Replace the toggleClass line with: toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer and body classes but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the _trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1665: function open(announceToSR) { ... setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); ... }
  Line 1685: function close(announceToSR) { ... setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function finds the _trigger element but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute. The trigger lacks correct initial state information for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1735: function init() { ... if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and dropdownMenu._closeInstance functions manage the panel visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger element dynamically. Only the initial state is set in register.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel visibility is toggled using the `hidden` attribute and CSS classes, but the trigger button's `aria-expanded` attribute is never updated to reflect the open or closed state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion panels are expanded and collapsed using `animateHeight` and the `hidden` attribute, but the trigger buttons do not have their `aria-expanded` attributes updated to indicate the panel state.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the code links the error message via `aria-describedby` but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field. When the field becomes valid, it does not clear the invalid state with `aria-invalid="false"`.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In `inlineValidator._renderResult` (else branch): add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The initialization code selects the navigation toggle button but does not initialize the `aria-expanded` attribute to `"false"`, leaving screen readers unaware of the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In `mobileNav.init`, after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add: `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Search combobox missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is given `role="combobox"` and `aria-controls`, but the required `aria-expanded` attribute is not initialized to `"false"` for the collapsed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In `suggestionListRenderer.mount`, after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add: `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the price values update visually, but the change is not announced via a live region, so screen reader users are not informed of the new prices.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In `pricing.setPeriod`, after updating the prices, add: `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced to screen readers, even though appropriate announcement strings exist in the `STRINGS` configuration object.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In `faqAccordion.open`, add: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question));`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close not announced
issuesFound.explanation: While opening the navigation announces "Navigation menu opened", closing the menu does not trigger a corresponding announcement, leaving users unaware that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In `mobileNav.close`, add: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Filter tabs missing aria-pressed or aria-selected state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons visually indicate the active filter with a CSS class, but they do not communicate this state programmatically using either `aria-pressed` (for toggle buttons) or `aria-selected` (for tab buttons).
issuesFound.fix: Line: In `productFilter._syncTabAriaStates`, inside the `forEach` loop, add: `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` or `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent disclosure trigger missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email field error message broken connection
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component is passed the incorrect prop name `error` instead of `errorMessage`, and the `errorId` prop is omitted. This prevents the error message from rendering and creates a broken aria-describedby reference on the input element pointing to a non-existent ID.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1248: Change `error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` to `errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} errorId="newsletter-email-error"`.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name field error message broken connection
issuesFound.explanation: FormGroup receives the prop `error` instead of the expected `errorMessage`, and lacks the `errorId` prop. Consequently, the error message is not displayed and the input's aria-describedby references an element that does not exist in the DOM.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1483: Change `error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}` to `errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined} errorId="contact-name-error"`.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm server error message rendering failure
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component expects a `message` prop to display text, but the implementation passes the error text as children instead. This results in the error message not being rendered to the user.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1278: Replace `<ErrorMessage id="newsletter-status" role="alert">Something went wrong. Please try again in a moment.</ErrorMessage>` with `<ErrorMessage id="newsletter-status" message="Something went wrong. Please try again in a moment." role="alert" />`.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm server error message rendering failure
issuesFound.explanation: ErrorMessage is used with child content instead of the required `message` prop, causing the submission error text to not be displayed to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1603: Replace `<ErrorMessage id="contact-server-error" role="alert">Submission failed...</ErrorMessage>` with `<ErrorMessage id="contact-server-error" message="Submission failed. Please check your connection and try again." role="alert" />`.

issuesFound.title: Filter toggle buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons track the `activeFilter` state to visually indicate selection but do not communicate this state to assistive technology via `aria-pressed`. issuesFound.title: ContactForm FormGroup incorrect error props for name field
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup is passed `error` prop instead of `errorMessage` and `errorId`, preventing error display and breaking the aria-describedby reference.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1886: Update FormGroup props.
  <FormGroup
    label="Full name"
    htmlFor="contact-name"
    required
    errorId="contact-name-error"
    errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}
  >
    <TextInput
      id="contact-name"
      name="name"
      type="text"
      autoComplete="name"
      required
      aria-required="true"
      value={name.state.value}
      onChange={(ev) => name.onChange(ev.target.value)}
      onBlur={name.onBlur}
      invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error}
      aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined}
    />
  </FormGroup>

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm field error message not rendered and broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup is passed the prop `error` but the component expects `errorMessage`. Additionally, `errorId` is not passed, so the ErrorMessage component is never rendered. However, the TextInput has aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" which references a non-existent element when errors occur, breaking the accessible description.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Change FormGroup props from `error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` to `errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} errorId="newsletter-email-error"` and ensure the TextInput receives aria-invalid.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section toggle buttons missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the button: `<button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>`

issuesFound.title: ErrorMessage component ignores children prop causing server error messages to not display
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component only renders the `message` prop and ignores `children`. When used with children for server errors, the component returns null (because message is undefined), so users never see or hear the error announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In ErrorMessage component definition, change the render to `{message || children}` or update the NewsletterForm and ContactForm usages to pass the error text via the `message` prop instead of as children.
